title: Node._relay_seen set grows unbounded, clears entire history at 128
description:
**Location:** python/src/lichen/node.py:386-389, 477-479

**Bug:** The `_relay_seen` set grows until it hits 128 entries, then clears entirely. This creates a window where immediately-after-clear, all previously-seen packets look fresh again, potentially causing relay loops.

**Trigger:** High-traffic mesh with >128 unique payloads in a short window, or an attacker who intentionally floods 128 unique packets then replays one from before the clear.

**Bad behavior:** After the set clears, a packet that was previously relayed (and should be suppressed as duplicate) gets relayed again, potentially causing broadcast storms or routing loops.

**Fix:** Use an LRU cache that evicts oldest entries one at a time rather than bulk-clearing, or use a time-based expiration.
